world-news.title: Hungarians vote as PM Orban faces toughest election challenge in years
world-news.summary: Hungary is holding a major parliamentary election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's 16-year control of the country. His rival Péter Magyar and his grassroots party are leading in polls, with record numbers of voters turning out to decide the nation's future direction.

world-news.title: Why did US-Iran talks end without an agreement in Pakistan?
world-news.summary: High-level talks between the United States and Iran in Pakistan have failed to produce a peace agreement to end their conflict. This is a major setback in international diplomacy, as these were the first direct negotiations between the two countries in years.

world-news.title: Oil tankers exit Strait of Hormuz amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
world-news.summary: Three large oil ships are leaving a critical shipping channel between the Persian Gulf and the Indian Ocean as the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran remains uncertain. This matters globally because this waterway is essential for world oil supplies, and any disruption affects energy prices everywhere.

world-news.title: 'We need real peace': Easter truce fails to lift grim mood in war-torn Ukraine
world-news.summary: A brief Easter ceasefire in Ukraine has ended without bringing hope for lasting peace, as fighting is expected to resume. The mood in Ukraine's Kharkiv region remains dark, with people exhausted by years of war with Russia.

world-news.title: Pakistan urges US and Iran to uphold ceasefire after talks end
world-news.summary: Pakistan's foreign minister is calling on the United States and Iran to stick to their ceasefire agreement even though their official peace talks have broken down. Pakistan is playing the role of peacemaker in this major international conflict.

world-news.title: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
world-news.summary: Hungarian voters are turning out in record numbers to decide if they want to remove Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power. Early polls suggest rival Péter Magyar's grassroots party has strong support.

world-news.title: Artemis II's moon-traveling astronauts return home to cheers after a record-breaking trip
world-news.summary: Astronauts from NASA and the Canadian Space Agency have successfully returned from a historic mission traveling to the moon, marking a major achievement for international space exploration. This demonstrates global cooperation in advancing human exploration beyond Earth.

world-news.title: US delegation leaves Pakistan without reaching Iran deal
world-news.summary: US Vice President JD Vance is departing Pakistan after failing to reach a peace agreement with Iran, saying Tehran rejected America's final offer. The collapse of these high-stakes negotiations raises concerns about the future stability of the Middle East.

world-news.title: Musician performs inside melting glacier to highlight climate crisis
world-news.summary: A Swiss musician performed inside a melting glacier cave in the Alps to raise awareness about climate change and how global warming is destroying ice. This artistic protest shows how the climate crisis is affecting Europe's most iconic landscapes.

world-news.title: Prosecutor probes abuse claims against California governor candidate Swalwell
world-news.summary: A federal prosecutor is investigating serious abuse allegations against Eric Swalwell, a leading Democrat running for California governor, though he denies the accusations. This case could significantly impact California's gubernatorial race and the state's political future.

automotive-ev-battery.title: It begins: electric articulated haul trucks reach series production
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo has started mass-producing its new A30 and A40 Electric haul trucks, marking the first time huge electric trucks of this size have moved from testing into regular production. This is a big deal because these massive vehicles are used in mining and construction, and switching them to electric power helps reduce pollution from heavy industry.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Boreal orders 20 electric hydrofoil ferries for Norway's coastline
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A Norwegian ferry company ordered 20 electric boats called hydrofoils from a Swedish manufacturer, creating the world's largest fleet of electric ferries. These fast, silent boats will carry passengers along Norway's coastal routes without producing any emissions.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia Beats Tesla To The Compact EV Sales Punch — In Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia's new EV2 is becoming the go-to affordable electric car for European shoppers, outselling Tesla in the budget-friendly compact EV market. The car combines nice features usually found in luxury vehicles with a price that regular people can afford.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla Sitting on Record Inventory
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla produced over 408,000 vehicles in Q1 2026 but only delivered about 358,000, leaving over 50,000 cars unsold. This large gap suggests that demand for Tesla vehicles might be slowing down, which could affect the company's sales strategy.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Review: 2025 Volkswagen ID.Buzz tags nostalgia, re-ups minivans
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen's new ID.Buzz electric minivan finally arrived in the U.S. with a large battery, good range (up to 234 miles), and fast charging (26 minutes for 10-80%). Starting at about $61,500, it combines the retro look of an old VW bus with modern electric technology.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Serbian Post adds 100 new electric vans to its fleet
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Serbia's postal service bought 100 electric delivery vans from an American company to replace old gas-powered trucks in Belgrade and other cities. This helps the country reduce pollution while delivering mail and packages.

automotive-ev-battery.title: March Sees Record New Plugin Vehicle Sales in Australia
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Australia hit a record high for selling plug-in hybrid and electric vehicles in March as gas and diesel prices keep climbing. More and more Australians are choosing electric cars because they're cheaper to fuel and run than traditional vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric, diesel, or CNG? MOOG tech lets you pick all of the above, on just ONE machine
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MOOG created technology that lets companies use electric power, diesel, or natural gas on the same vehicle, giving fleets flexibility when switching to cleaner energy. This approach costs less and has less risk than buying completely different vehicles for each fuel type.

automotive-ev-battery.title: DHL Express Norway adopts Spirii charging platform
automotive-ev-battery.summary: DHL's Norwegian division partnered with a Danish company to improve its electric van charging network. Better charging infrastructure helps delivery companies like DHL run more electric vehicles efficiently.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The Chevy Equinox EV is 20% off right now with a $10,000 discount
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chevy is offering a huge $10,000 discount (20% off) on the Equinox EV, one of the cheapest electric SUVs you can buy. This deal makes an already affordable EV even more accessible for families looking to go electric.

tech-ai.title: The Netherlands is the first European country to approve Tesla's supervised Full Self-Driving
tech-ai.summary: Tesla's self-driving technology just got approved in the Netherlands, making it the first European country to allow it on roads. This is a big deal because it could lead to more countries in Europe allowing Tesla's Full Self-Driving system. Since Tesla's main European office is in Amsterdam, it makes sense that the Netherlands approved it first.

tech-ai.title: NASA Artemis II splashes down in Pacific Ocean in 'perfect' landing for Moon mission
tech-ai.summary: NASA's Artemis II spacecraft successfully returned to Earth and landed in the Pacific Ocean near San Diego after traveling farther from Earth than any previous mission. The perfect landing means the mission was a complete success. This is an important step for NASA's plans to eventually send people back to the Moon.

tech-ai.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski's dad was a CIA operative
tech-ai.summary: A reporter discovered that an AI companion living inside a cute baby deer plushie was spreading false conspiracy theories about the musician Mitski's father. This shows how AI companions can accidentally (or intentionally) spread misinformation to people who talk to them. It's a weird but important reminder that AI systems can share incorrect information.

tech-ai.title: Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
tech-ai.summary: Ascend Elements, a company that recycles lithium-ion batteries (the kind used in electric cars and phones), went bankrupt after losing government funding and facing tough competition. This matters because we need battery recycling companies to handle old batteries responsibly and recover valuable materials. The company's failure shows how challenging the battery recycling business is right now.

tech-ai.title: Pokémon Champions is off to a rough start
tech-ai.summary: The new Pokémon Champions game on Nintendo Switch has launched with lots of bugs, including problems with basic battle mechanics that mess up gameplay. While some bugs have already been fixed, the bigger issue is that the game's design and balance don't work well yet. Hopefully the developers will improve it as they update the game.

tech-ai.title: AMC will stream 'The Audacity' premiere in 21 parts on TikTok
tech-ai.summary: AMC is trying something new by breaking up a TV show premiere into 21 short clips and releasing them on TikTok to build excitement. Some people think this is a smart way to get young people interested in the show, while others say it's just copying Quibi, an old failed streaming service that tried the same thing. It's an interesting experiment in how entertainment companies are adapting to short-form video platforms.

tech-ai.title: Walmart-owned Flipkart, Amazon are squeezing India's quick commerce startups
tech-ai.summary: Amazon and Flipkart (which Walmart owns) are expanding into smaller Indian cities and offering huge discounts, making it really hard for smaller quick-delivery companies to compete. Quick commerce means getting stuff delivered super fast, usually within 30 minutes. The bigger companies' aggressive expansion is putting smaller startups at risk of going out of business.

tech-ai.title: You don't have to spend more than $50 on a great USB-C dock for your Switch 2
tech-ai.summary: Nintendo Switch 2 needs special accessories to work properly, and good USB-C docks (devices that connect your Switch to a TV or charger) are now available for under $50. Nintendo changed how the Switch 2 connects to accessories, so third-party companies had to completely redesign their products. The good news is you can get quality docks without spending a lot of money.

tech-ai.title: Google's latest Nest Doorbells just hit their lowest prices of the year
tech-ai.summary: Google's battery-powered Nest Doorbell video camera is now on sale for $129.99, which is the cheapest price it's been all year. Video doorbells let you see who's at your door even when you're not home, which is helpful for preventing porch pirates from stealing packages. This deal is available at Amazon and Best Buy right now.

tech-ai.title: The Artemis II mission has ended. Where does NASA go from here?
tech-ai.summary: Now that Artemis II has successfully completed its mission around the Moon, NASA is looking ahead to the next steps in getting people back to the Moon. The mission proved that NASA's technology and planning work, but there's still lots of hard work ahead. Scientists and engineers are already thinking about what comes next for Moon exploration.

finance-markets.title: Commodity traders lost ‘billions’ in early days of Iran war
finance-markets.summary: Firms that normally profit from volatility were caught out by sudden rise in energy prices, new report finds

finance-markets.title: Fees for seas: a history of taxing waterways
finance-markets.summary: From Ottoman Sultans to Danish kings, Iran’s toll system for Strait of Hormuz revives an old maritime fight

finance-markets.title: The historic Iranian palaces caught up in US-Israeli strikes
finance-markets.summary: World Heritage sites including Tehran’s Golestan Palace were damaged in bombings

finance-markets.title: Social media is for picking up vibes, not information
finance-markets.summary: Users trust it even less than they do mainstream news outlets

finance-markets.title: Switzerland’s Zug becomes bolt-hole for Gulf-based wealth
finance-markets.summary: Small Swiss town is welcoming individuals and companies seeking refuge from war in the Middle East

finance-markets.title: Brexiters turn their backs on Trump after Iran chaos
finance-markets.summary: Nigel Farage and Kemi Badenoch distance themselves from the US president, having once celebrated his example

finance-markets.title: Robinhood excludes some prediction markets over manipulation fears
finance-markets.summary: US broker says it is ‘very focused on insider trading’ as it pushes into fast-growing sector

finance-markets.title: Wall Street banks set to report $40bn trading haul as Iran war rekindles volatility
finance-markets.summary: Five largest US lenders expected to unveil highest combined trading revenues since at least 2014

finance-markets.title: India Forex Regulator Criticizes Banks’ Rupee Arbitrage Trades
finance-markets.summary: A senior Reserve Bank of India official criticized foreign-exchange market makers for their role in aggravating the rupee’s weakness during the Middle East tensions, as the regulator keeps up its tough messaging stance in its defense of the currency.

finance-markets.title: US and Iran Have Failed to Reach an Agreement, JD Vance Says
finance-markets.summary:
The US and Iran failed to reach an agreement after marathon talks in Pakistan on the weekend, a major setback to finding a lasting resolution to the six-week war in the Middle East.

Vice President JD Vance said negotiators will return to the US without a deal after Iran failed to give a commitment it would not seek a nuclear weapon. (Source: Bloomberg)

science-space.title: La NASA da la bienvenida a la Tierra a los exploradores lunares de Artemis II, quienes batieron récords
science-space.summary: Los primeros astronautas en viajar a la Luna en más de medio siglo han regresado a la Tierra tras una misión que ha establecido nuevos récords a bordo del vuelo de prueba Artemis II de la NASA. Los astronautas de la NASA Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover y Christina Koch, y el astronauta Jeremy Hansen de la […]

science-space.title: Artemis II Splashes Down
science-space.summary: This image from April 10, 2026, captures NASA’s Orion spacecraft, with its parachutes deployed, seconds before splashdown in the Pacific Ocean. The Artemis II crew accomplished many milestones on their nearly 10-day mission, surpassing the Apollo 13 record for farthest crewed spaceflight and capturing views of the far side of the Moon. Under Artemis, NASA […]

science-space.title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
science-space.summary: Windshaper A large WindShaper fan array is available for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research.  The WindShaper is ideal for generating arbitrary wind gradients and wind gusts via a simple Python API.  A companion WindProbe is also available for quick surveys of flows.  The WindProbe utilizes the lab’s OptiTrack motion capture system to extract the […]

science-space.title: I Am Artemis: Dan Florez
science-space.summary: Listen to this audio excerpt from Dan Florez, test director for NASA’s Exploration Ground Systems Program: At 1:47 a.m. EST November 16, 2022, as the Artemis I engines ignited, NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman and Dan Florez, NASA test director for the agency’s Exploration Ground Systems, watched from Kennedy’s Launch Control Center roof as the midnight […]

science-space.title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
science-space.summary: On Episode 205 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discuss the flight of Artemis 2.

science-space.title: NEAF 2026 returns to New York this weekend with all-star astronaut lineup and 'world's largest' space expo
science-space.summary: Thousands of enthusiasts, professionals and curious skywatchers will gather this weekend for the world’s largest and most spectacular astronomy and space expo.

science-space.title: 'They’re not shipping cows up to Mars': 'For All Mankind' creators talk scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5 (interview)
science-space.summary: We chat with "For All Mankind" creators Ben Nedivi and Matt Wolbert about season 5's dramatic arc, scientific accuracy, and real-world parallels.

science-space.title: NASA took this camera gear to space aboard Artemis 2, and you can own it, too!
science-space.summary: Artemis 2 is the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years, but the cameras onboard are surprisingly down-to-earth. Here's how to get your hands on the gear that has brought us so many stunning shots of space.

science-space.title: Artemis 2 spies a crescent Earth above the moon's horizon | Space photo of the day for April 10, 2026
science-space.summary: Earth shines above the lunar horizon in a new Artemis 2 photo.

energy-climate.title: The Hydrogen Stream: BMW unveils tank for iX5 Hydrogen
energy-climate.summary: BMW says its new hydrogen tank enables longer range and flexible production, while South Korea has revealed plans to develop large-scale liquid hydrogen storage systems.

energy-climate.title: Key takeaways from SolarEx Istanbul
energy-climate.summary: The 18th SolarEx Istanbul showcased Türkiye’s expanding solar ecosystem, bringing together hundreds of exhibitors and reinforcing its role as a rising global hub for PV manufacturing and innovation. At the same time, strong growth in distributed solar and emerging battery storage highlights both the market’s potential and its key challenges, including regulatory constraints, grid limits, and increasing global competition.

energy-climate.title: Turkish manufacturer presents photovoltaic water heater
energy-climate.summary: Adana-based WHS introduced a PV-powered water heating system that operates without an inverter, using 1.6 kW of direct DC solar input with integrated MPPT. The system supports hybrid operation with a 2 kW AC backup, runs at extra-low voltage of less than 50 V, delivers around 3 kWh/day of thermal energy, and heats water to 65–85 C for residential and small commercial use.

energy-climate.title: ‘I joined the management team the day after returning from maternity leave’
energy-climate.summary: This week Women in Solar+ Europe gives voice to Ilse Cappelle, Head of Marketing & E-commerce at Netherlands' Libra Energy. She explains how returning from maternity leave directly into a leadership role showed her the company’s strong support for career growth and work-life balance.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: What STAT readers think about nutrition education in med school
health-biotech.summary: “Is ‘lack of education’ really what we think the problem is in prevention care?” a STAT reader asks.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: Sports betting is creating a twofold public health crisis for some young men
health-biotech.summary: “Even if not everybody is losing a ton of money right now, you're creating this culture of large numbers of gamblers,” Isaac Rose-Berman says on the First Opinion Podcast.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Up and down the ladder: The latest comings and goings
health-biotech.summary: From new hires to departures, promotions and transfers, here are the latest comings and goings in the pharmaceutical industry.

health-biotech.title: Trump administration admits a glaring error in its accusations about New York health care fraud
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration this week acknowledged it made a significant error in figures it used to help justify a fraud probe into New York’s Medicaid program

health-biotech.summary: In this week's STATus Report, host Alex Hogan dives into how sports betting apps hook users — and how he himself fell for some too-good-to-be-true promotions.

health-biotech.summary: Welcome back to another edition of Endpoints Weekly. Two of the year’s major oncology conferences are coming up soon: AACR starting April 17, and ASCO at the end of May. We’re also hosting our New ...

health-biotech.title: FDA rejects Replimune cancer therapy, saying company didn't resolve trial doubts
health-biotech.summary:
The FDA has once again rejected Replimune’s oncolytic virus therapy to treat advanced melanoma.

 The Massachusetts-based drugmaker had been pushing for a reconsideration by the agency since the therapy was first rejected last July. At ...

crypto-web3.title: XRP drops to $1.33 as bitcoin weakness pulls down majors
crypto-web3.summary: Sudden selloff triggered liquidation-style move, with weak recovery reinforcing bearish structure despite compressed volatility setup.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin, broader market flat as U.S.-Iran negotiations begin
crypto-web3.summary: The market rose over the week after a two-week ceasefire was announced, triggering a derivatives short squeeze that wiped out over $430 million in bearish positions.

crypto-web3.title: Iran war oil-price shock revives inflation trade and a new stablecoin play
crypto-web3.summary: As oil shocks revive investor anxiety, stablecoins solved payments, but not purchasing power, says Michael Ashton, who's USDi token aims to fix that.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto perpetuals predict the direction of Wall Street’s Monday open with 89% accuracy, data shows
crypto-web3.summary: Over half (57%) of Monday market opens is s already reflected in crypto markets' perpetual futures products.

crypto-web3.title: Bitwise files second amendment to Hyperliquid ETF, adds Wintermute and Flowdesk as trading counterparties
crypto-web3.summary: The filing comes a day after Bitwise Europe listed a physically-backed Hyperliquid staking ETP on Deutsche Börse Xetra.

crypto-web3.title: Ether Machine and Dynamix mutually scrap SPAC merger, blame ‘unfavorable market conditions’
crypto-web3.summary: Under the Termination Agreement effective April 8, an unnamed "Payor" likely connected to The Ether Machine must pay Dynamix $50 million within 15 days.

crypto-web3.title: US government moves bitcoin possibly linked to steroid distribution conspiracy
crypto-web3.summary: The U.S. government currently holds roughly 328,000 bitcoins, worth more than $22 billion at current prices, among other crypto assets.

crypto-web3.title: AI agents reshaping crypto development, trading and risk, with Cambrian and Ethereum Foundation
crypto-web3.summary: Builders and researchers discuss how AI agents are transforming crypto, from development and trading to entirely new financial systems.

crypto-web3.title: How Telegram plans to turn crypto into a global payments network
crypto-web3.summary: Nikola Plecas explains how TON is building crypto payments into Telegram and where the biggest opportunities for adoption lie.

crypto-web3.title: World decreasing WLD token unlock rate by over 40% starting in July
crypto-web3.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a co-founder of Tools for Humanity, the chief developer behind the World project.

startups-vc.title: Walmart-owned Flipkart, Amazon are squeezing India’s quick commerce startups
startups-vc.summary: Flipkart's ongoing expansion beyond major cities and heavy discounting is raising risks for India's quick commerce startups, analysts say.

startups-vc.title: Kalshi wins temporary pause in Arizona criminal case
startups-vc.summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission announced Friday that it has won a temporary restraining order preventing Arizona from pursuing its criminal case against Kalshi.

startups-vc.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
startups-vc.summary: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's software revolution, deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech, and candid conversations about how AI is rewriting the global music and anime industries.

startups-vc.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
startups-vc.summary: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

startups-vc.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
startups-vc.summary: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

startups-vc.title: Final 2 days to save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 ticket
startups-vc.summary: Ticket discounts of up to $500 will end tomorrow, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. After that, prices for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 go up again. Miss this, and you’ll be paying more for the same access to one of the most anticipated tech events of the year. Register now to lock in these savings.

startups-vc.title: Global Investors Help Boost Latin America’s Late-Stage Funding Boom In Q1
startups-vc.summary: Startups in Latin America raised a combined $1.03 billion across seed- and growth-stage deals in the three-month period ending March 31 — up year over year, but down from Q4 2025.

startups-vc.title: Most Active And Highest-Spending Startup Investors Diverged In Q1
startups-vc.summary: The investors backing the highest number of startup rounds in a record-setting Q1 were mostly not the ones writing the biggest checks. And the ones funding the largest deals were not the most prolific dealmakers. To see who ranked high for deal counts and totals, we looked at active investors across multiple metrics.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Credit Card Backed By Mineral Rights, Flying Ferries, And A Foundation AI Model For Plants
startups-vc.summary: In a quarter when nearly two-thirds of global venture capital went to just four companies, it’s easy to lose track of the many other companies getting funding to tackle interesting problems. We spotted five companies in just the past month working on issues from cleaner ferries and trains to foundational AI for the crop industry.

startups-vc.title: Just Because We Can: The Strategic Risks Of Automating Everything
startups-vc.summary: While AI and automation can be powerful, many applications use complex global systems to solve simple problems that could be handled locally. Guest author Itay Sagie shares three risks of undisciplined automation of everything, urging more thoughtful and disciplined use of technology.

cybersecurity.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
cybersecurity.summary: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

cybersecurity.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
cybersecurity.summary: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n

cybersecurity.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
cybersecurity.summary: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines wh

cybersecurity.title: Kimwolf Botnet Swamps Anonymity Network I2P
cybersecurity.summary: For the past week, the massive "Internet of Things" (IoT) botnet known as Kimwolf has been disrupting the The Invisible Internet Project (I2P), a decentralized, encrypted communications network designed to anonymize and secure online communications. I2P users started reporting disruptions in the network around the same time the Kimwolf botmasters began relying on it to evade takedown attempts against the botnet's control servers.

cybersecurity.title: CPUID Breach Distributes STX RAT via Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
cybersecurity.summary:
Unknown threat actors compromised CPUID ("cpuid[.]com"), a website that hosts popular hardware monitoring tools like CPU-Z, HWMonitor, HWMonitor Pro, and PerfMonitor, for less than 24 hours to serve malicious executables for the software and deploy a remote access trojan called STX RAT.
The incident lasted from approximately April 9, 15:00 UTC, to about April 10, 10:00 UTC, with

cybersecurity.title: Adobe Patches Actively Exploited Acrobat Reader Flaw CVE-2026-34621
cybersecurity.summary:
Adobe has released emergency updates to fix a critical security flaw in Acrobat Reader that has come under active exploitation in the wild.
The vulnerability, assigned the CVE identifier CVE-2026-34621, carries a CVSS score of 8.6 out of 10.0. Successful exploitation of the flaw could allow an attacker to run malicious code on affected installations.
It has been described as

cybersecurity.title: Browser Extensions Are the New AI Consumption Channel That No One Is Talking About
cybersecurity.summary:
While much of the discussion on AI security centers around protecting ‘shadow’ AI and GenAI consumption, there's a wide-open window nobody's guarding: AI browser extensions. 
A new report from LayerX exposes just how deep this blind spot goes, and why AI extensions may be the most dangerous AI threat surface in your network that isn't on anyone's

cybersecurity.title: Google Rolls Out DBSC in Chrome 146 to Block Session Theft on Windows
cybersecurity.summary:
Google has made Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) generally available to all Windows users of its Chrome web browser, months after it began testing the security feature in open beta.
The public availability is currently limited to Windows users on Chrome 146, with macOS expansion planned in an upcoming Chrome release.
"This project represents a significant

cybersecurity.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
cybersecurity.summary:
A previously undocumented threat cluster dubbed UAT-10362 has been attributed to spear-phishing campaigns targeting Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to deploy a new Lua-based malware called LucidRook.

asia-pacific.title: Colleague Skill: AI job fears in China set off viral spread of supposed ability harvester
asia-pacific.summary:
An open-source artificial intelligence project aimed at harvesting human capabilities into reusable AI “skills” has gone viral in China, gaining traction as a meme among the country’s uneasy young workers as they face growing job insecurity amid rapid AI advances.
Supposedly, certain skills of luminaries such as Steve Jobs, spiritual figures like Gautama Buddha and ordinary office workers have been extracted into digital form and uploaded online, making these skills – such as Jobs’ product...

asia-pacific.title: AI pessimism is a luxury the Global South cannot afford
asia-pacific.summary:
No Ethiopian, Pakistani, Indian, Brazilian or other serious policymaker believes artificial intelligence will solve corruption or improve governance overnight. National policies such as Digital Ethiopia 2030, Pakistan’s National AI Policy 2025 or other initiatives in Chile, Argentina and Colombia consider AI as a means to enhance service delivery in healthcare, education, agriculture, taxation and disaster management, rather than as institutional reform.
These are practical applications....

asia-pacific.title: Forget Hong Kong and Singapore, Philippines housing is least affordable: survey
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong and Singapore routinely top global rankings of unaffordable cities. Yet it is the residents of Manila, Colombo and Yangon who are far more likely to tell pollsters they cannot afford shelter.
The Philippines recorded the world’s highest share of people reporting difficulty affording housing in a new survey from US-based firm Gallup, with 55 per cent saying they had struggled to pay for shelter in the past 12 months.
Sri Lanka followed at 54 per cent, Myanmar at 49 per cent and Thailand

asia-pacific.title: Semiconductor leap: China looks to next-gen ‘2D chip’ with 1,000-fold growth speed
asia-pacific.summary:
Chinese scientists say they have developed a wafer-scale 2D semiconductor growth method with 1,000 times faster growth, paving the way for industry advances.
The surging demand for high‑performance, low‑power chips driven by AI and large-language models has intensified the search for next‑generation semiconductor technologies.
Moore’s Law predicted a doubling of semiconductor capacity every two years but as chip dimensions continue to shrink, physical limitations make further performance scaling

asia-pacific.title: How China’s fake Titanic sank like a stone – and became a symbol of local excess
asia-pacific.summary:
When Su Shaojun launched an audacious plan to build a life-size replica of the Titanic, he felt he was on to a sure-fire winner. After all, James Cameron’s iconic 1997 film had been a worldwide hit – including in China, where it had grossed over US$200 million.
At a press event to unveil the project in 2014, Su invited the actor Bernard Hill – who played the captain of the Titanic in the film – to appear alongside him as he told the assembled reporters in Hong Kong that his company aimed to...

asia-pacific.title: Minister warns Malaysia will enter ‘critical period’ for fuel supply by June
asia-pacific.summary:
Malaysia is expected to experience a critical moment with regards to fuel supply by June, according to Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.
He said the government is implementing measures to address the global fuel shortage amid the Middle East conflict, including exploring alternative raw materials.
“June and July will be a very critical period in ensuring fuel supplies are available,” Akmal Nasrullah said after attending an event in Johor Bahru.

asia-pacific.summary: Survey findings complicate the notion that U.S.-China economic policy presents a binary choice between the two options.

asia-pacific.title: Friendly Strangers: How Two Peoples Across the Taiwan Strait Are Drifting Apart
asia-pacific.summary: The populations of China and Taiwan are drifting apart in ways that will be structurally hard to reverse.

asia-pacific.summary: The route has proven its value in times of crisis. Can it evolve into a primary artery of Eurasian trade?

asia-pacific.summary: For a grouping that until recently thrived on high-profile summitry, the Quad’s present silence is striking to say the least.
